title: Scientists Say No to Removing Climate Info
summary: A group of scientists called the National Academies of Sciences made a book to help judges understand science in court cases. This book has a new part about climate change, which explains how human activities are warming the Earth. Some politicians from certain states did not like this and asked the scientists to take out the climate change part. The scientists said no because they followed the same rules for all parts of the book. They want judges to learn about climate science to make fair decisions in court. Other scientists who wrote different parts of the book also supported keeping the climate change information, saying it is important for judges to know the facts without political interference. This shows that science should be shared openly, even when some people disagree.

keywords.explanation: A group of scientists who study and share important science information.
Changes in Earth's weather patterns, often caused by human activities like burning fuels.
People in court who make decisions based on laws and facts.
People who work in government and make rules or decisions.
A written guide that helps judges learn about science topics.

questions.question: What is the National Academies of Sciences?
What new part did the book for judges include?
Who asked the scientists to remove the climate change part?
Did the scientists agree to remove the climate change part?
Why did the scientists say no to removing the part?
options: They followed the same rules for all parts
They did not like the politicians
They wanted to make a new book
They thought it was too hard
questions.correct_answer: They followed the same rules for all parts

questions.question: What did other scientists who wrote parts of the book do?
options: Supported keeping the climate change info
questions.correct_answer: Supported keeping the climate change info

background_read: This article is about a science book made to help judges in court. Judges need to understand science to make fair decisions, especially in cases about the environment. Some politicians wanted to remove information about climate change from the book, but scientists said no because they believe in sharing accurate science. This shows how science can be important in law and why it should not be hidden by politics.
Article_Structure: WHO: National Academies of Sciences and politicians from some states
WHAT: A disagreement about including climate change information in a book for judges
WHERE: In the United States, involving government and science groups
WHY: To ensure judges have accurate science facts without political interference

perspectives.description: Scientists believe climate change information is important for judges to learn and should be included based on fair rules.
Some politicians think the climate change part is one-sided and should be removed from the book.
